reviews: Out on a bingeing spree to mark the start to this Deepavali long weekend! This petite bowl of laksa at $2.80 is perfect for me with just a mild level of spiciness since I’ve got a low tolerance towards spicy food; and the small portion is great if you are feeling not that hungry but still feel like eating something, before dinner time.
Hardly we can find a $2.50 bowl of Laksa nowadays. 63 Laksa broth doesn't has a rich coconut flavour but more with the dry shrimp taste which is quite flavourful and easy to drink

The laksa ($2.50) is served in a traditional looking porcelain bowl together with a porcelain spoon. Although not a very big bowl, it is filled to the brim with shortened thick bee hoon, uniformly sliced fish cake, bean sprouts and cockles.

other_info.address: 20 Ghim Moh Road #01-20 Ghim Moh Market & Food Centre Singapore 270020

reviews.author: CS* Chia
reviews.text:
Stall had a short queue and an inviting decor that caught my eye. The atmosphere was vibrant yet casual, perfect for a quick bite.

I decided to try their laksa, and I was amazed at how affordable it was. A small bowl costs only $2.80, which is quite hard to believe for such a popular dish.

The laksa was served with my choice of noodles, complemented by slices of fish cakes and bean sprouts. While it wasn’t a large portion, the flavors certainly packed a punch.

The standout feature of this bowl was definitely the laksa gravy sauce. It was rich and flavorful, providing that authentic taste I was craving. Although the bowl was small and not overly filling, it satisfied my laksa cravings perfectly, allowing me to save room for other delicious offerings from nearby stalls.

Overall, I highly recommend the Laksa Noodle Stall at Ghim Moh Food Market. It’s a humble yet satisfying option for anyone looking for a quick and affordable laksa fix.

reviews.author: Mark Yeo
reviews.text: Just nice - a small portioned bowl of laksa with thick bee hoon and some cockles, chilli and laksa leaves. Broth is light, sufficiently lemak, tasty but not too salty. Stall operator is friendly and has systematic order taking questions for customers who are not familiar. The stall also otah has optional add on. I noticed many regular customers getting their lunch packed and can see its popularity.

reviews.author: Maximilian Jackson
reviews.text:
63 Laksa at Ghim Moh Market: A Laksa Lover's Dream

If you're a laksa enthusiast searching for the ultimate culinary experience, look no further than 63 Laksa at Ghim Moh Market. This hidden gem has quickly become a local favorite, and for good reason.

The laksa here is simply divine. The broth is rich and flavorful, with a perfect balance of spicy, tangy, and creamy notes. The noodles are cooked to perfection, offering a satisfying chew. The assortment of ingredients, including cockles, prawns, and tofu, adds a delightful texture and depth of flavor.

What truly sets 63 Laksa apart is the attention to detail. Every element of the dish is thoughtfully prepared, ensuring that each bite is a burst of flavor. The generous portion size is another bonus.

If you're lucky enough to visit during peak hours, be prepared for a short wait, as this place is always packed. But trust me, it's worth the wait. 63 Laksa at Ghim Moh Market is a must-visit for any food lover in Singapore.

Don’t forget to order the otah! A perfect complement to the Laksa.

reviews.author: Shawn Lim
reviews.text:
Simply lovely.
I initially ordered the fried horfun which was from another store but was sat around this shop.
I couldn’t resist and ordered a mini bowl.
The laksa isn’t too cloying nor oily and can be eaten with just a spoon. Noodles were done just nice and it’s so damn fragrant.

reviews.author: L L
reviews.text: There was a long queue when I arrived, but the uncle was very patient as I fumbled with the mobile payment. When I brought the Laksa to the office, I realised the thoughtful detail of short noodles, making it easy to eat with a spoon. The laksa itself was delicious, and I'd definitely recommend it to anyone in the area.

reviews: Butterfly Fritter @ Delisnacks 德利食, 20 Ghim Moh Road, Ghim Moh Market & Food Centre #01-23. Experienced some fried butterfly fritter for breakfast. Nice crisp and sweet but found it quite oily as well.

other_info.address: 20 Ghim Moh Road #01-23 Ghim Moh Market & Food Centre Singapore 270020

reviews.author: Flo Yeow
reviews.text: Standard franchise that you can probably find in many other places. That also ensures a certain quality control for the products they serve across the country. They have good dough fritters you tiao here, and we always get their butterfly bun as well. Great for that morning breakfast snack you need, to go with your morning soya bean milk or coffee.

reviews.author: Capture Asia Photography
reviews.text:
Delicious snack! Even though i picked the last carrot cake and is still warm! The uncle that served me was kind and super friendly. Cheers

Taste and service is always subjective.

reviews.author: Botak
reviews.text:
We will normally just go for their goreng pisang since it's value for money at $1.20 for such a huge piece.

The rest of the stuff,like youtiao, butterfly, are reasonable tasting and also priced at $1.20 market price.

reviews.author: SK Ho
reviews.text: Ordered you tiao, fried green beans and banana fritters. So crispy . Love all of them!

reviews.author: Donald Chen
reviews.text: Good tasty snacks like it’s popular You Tiao and Butterfly. One of the better ones that you can find.

reviews: Freshly made appam available in original, egg or cheese, paired with red jaggery sugar and freshly grated coconut.
Previously known as Heaven's Indian Curry, this stall specialises in 2 items: Appam and Putu Mayam. The Appam is made on the spot.
Princess Appam (S$3) Cooked with egg and butter, topped with cheese, eat with sugar or orange sugar.

other_info.address: 20 Ghim Moh Road #01-26 Ghim Moh Market & Food Centre Singapore 270020

reviews.author: Alvin Choo
reviews.text:
I found out about this stall from reddit and it did not disappoint!

You have to order from the right side of the stall while the queue to wait for the food is on the left. Some items are served quite quickly, but others are cooked to order.

I really enjoyed the princess and the royal. Will be back again to eat the food! You have to eat it quickly though, while they are all still crispy.

reviews.author: Paul Lui
reviews.text:
Best Appam I have tasted.
Very very freshly made so have to be patience and wait. Then must eat immediately 😋.
Highly recommended and a must try when you are in this food court.

reviews.author: Elbert Damian
reviews.text:
The Princess, The Royal
.
Princess is an apam with butter, egg and cheese accompanied with light curry, grated coconut and sugar. Base alone already has flavor but adding the complement brings extra flavor and completes it.
While royal comes with masala potato which gives another complexity to the flavour and along with the complement really giving a flavourful taste.
.
#sgfoodies #sgfood #heavens #apambalik #tosai
IG: @betzdamz

reviews.author: L
reviews.text:
Saw the good reviews so decided to try for lunch and got the royal for  think 5.50$ which is okay.
Very nice dish. Crispy thosai with a tasty filling and the dips were flavorful and good too. Definitely want to come back here and try the appam next.

reviews.author: Elaine C
reviews.text: Popular stall for South Indian dosai and appam with tasty condiments—sugar, coconut, curry.

reviews: Long Queue Mee Siam @ $4/5. This is Chinese mee siam, not as tangy & sour. But taste very good.
Satay Beehoon. Everyone queue for their mee siam but their satay beehoon is not bad too.
Decent bowl of Mee Siam ($3.00) The uncle thoughtfully helps to cut the bee hoon for easier consumption.

reviews.author: Athena
reviews.text: Mee Siam nice. Generous portion of dried shrimps on the side and  you can also taste the coconut in the gravy . Not spicy unless you put sambal chilli.  Not the typical mee siam. The stall owners prompt and polite. They also know what their regulars like . Very warm

reviews.author: Alvin Choo
reviews.text:
I really enjoyed the mee siam here, because it was more tangy than other mee siams that I have eaten, and reminded me of a stall at Jurong East interchange back when I was still in primary school more than 20+ years ago!

I highly encourage giving the mee siam a try because it is different from those from Malay stalls!

reviews.author: Paul Young
reviews.text: This is really great. Lots of Mee. The soup was delicious and typical thickness and not watery. Finished it quickly. The 5 bucks one is big. Actually, I think 4 dollars is good enough. If there is something to add, it will be fried chicken. Haha.

reviews.author: Jeffrey Ng
reviews.text: A delicious Mee Siam. Taste like the Indian type , with coconut and peanuts on the gravy soup, love the addition of the crispy ‘Hae pi’ small tiny shrimp, my all time favorites, had it for a continuous 4 days, yummy good 👍👍👍😋😋😋, they do sell satay bee Hoon on certain days, but, yet to try

reviews.author: Kriz A
reviews.text: This is truly a gem that we accidentally chanced upon. We were there on a Thursday and there was only 3 persons in the queue infront of us so we have no reference- longer queues better food. But we decided to try out and it was amazing! The Mee Siam gravy was rich and yet not too heavy and it’s loaded with tiny fried shrimps, tau pok and Gu Chai. It’s so delicious we decided to return on a Saturday, 2 days later for their Satay Beehoon too! Satay Beehoon is only sold on Fridays, Saturdays, Sundays and public holidays. And we were once again blown away. For $5.50, there’s loads of ingredients and they r thickly sliced! The satay sauce is very well balanced in taste and the portion is great for the value we paid. But we had to wait in line for close to 20 mins. There were 8 people ahead of us. Still it’s well worth the wait. We will definitely be back!!

reviews: Durians!!! Durians!!!! My turn to have durians! Since my hubby din buy for me. I get it myself!!!
D13 for dessert after lunch on Boxing Day. #durian

other_info.address: 20 Ghim Moh Road #01-125 Ghim Moh Market & Food Centre Singapore 270020

reviews.author: Cello Febrian
reviews.text:
Bought 3 boxes of MSW yesterday at $40. Terrible.

Out of the 3 boxes, only 1 box tastes acceptable, but one seed texture like eating potato.

Rest of the 2 boxes, not fragrance, potato texture, one of the seeds a bit sourish.

Guess I am the only unlucky ones with lousy durians despite a general good ratings

reviews.author: sein cho
reviews.text: Last night, we went to Ghim Moh market to have durian . It’s almost 8pm and the shop we intended to eat was not willing to host us as they were going to close soon. So we walked around and seen this shop and some customers are still having durian. We asked whether still can dine in and they allowed us , prepared tables and chairs that are already folded . They gave as free water cups and the durian….. best MSW we ever tried! 😋

reviews.author: Jason L
reviews.text:
Impromptu trip to Ghim Moh to look for Durian, most of the stall already sold out their durian as it was close to 5pm. Happened to chance upon this stall and just nice they still have some D15 and D17 available, decided to get few kilos each.

Price is reasonable, seller is friendly, they do replaced for you if some of the fruits is not ripe / spoilt.

Both durian taste great, I would say value for money as it is fresh, much better then those pre packed durian from hypermarket.

Will I still come back to their stall ? Maybe yes maybe no because there are plenty of durian seller at ghim moh for me to try out still.

reviews.author: Naeson Menon
reviews.text: Some of the best JB XO & MSW I’ve had in Singapore thus far. Very honest seller - first durian was dry he let us choose new one. Really apprrciate the service and price - very reasonable for ghim moh area

reviews.author: Chewielicious T
reviews.text:
Got some MSW for $32/kg - quite pricey but worth every single cent. Met the boss Raymond and he shared that it’s better to go on-site and self collect, better then delivery because you get to pick the durians.

There wasn’t much left when I arrived around 3pm, but he swears by the quality of the durian he’s selling. Ended up paying ~$230 for 4 vacuumed boxes from 4 durians.

Lo and behold; shared it with a friend who was visiting from Japan who absolutely hates durian. After some encouragement she decided to give durian another try.

Wow, after her first bite, she said “ohmygod! I love this! There’s a tinge of bitterness that makes this so delicious! I never knew durians tasted like this! ”

Well; guess Lele’s durian converted a durian hater to a durian fan - worth every single cent then!

Thank you boss Raymond!

reviews: Decent Left: Pan Fried Salmon with Veggies in Pink Sauce Right: Salmon Donburi (Both $7.50 nett each)
Salmon donburi SGD 7.50 This is nice
dishes: Pan Fried Salmon with Veggies in Pink Sauce

google.google_name: 20 Ghim Moh Road Market & Food Centre

reviews.author: TPH
reviews.text:
I like to suggest that Ghim Moh food n hawker centre is a good or better places to go during breakfast n lunch
Has a nice variety of delicious hawker food congregated under one roof
Ample paid parking lots.
Next block has Aircond shop to buy fresh fruits n vegetables till 10 pm at night.
Can find  stalls selling yummy food:
1.Whampoa soya bean drinks
2.Delisnacks- good Yu tiao, butterfly bun, curry puff etc( hve good oil to fry)
3.Tong Fong Fatt Hainanese boneless chicken rice( chicken is smooth, young n tender with good soup)
4.Chuan Kee duck rice
5.Thye Hong Handmade fishball noodles
6..Ghim Moh carrot cake
7.Jiu Jiang Shao la roast meats
8.Teck Hin Chee Cheong fan( fantastic taste)
9.Hock Soon carrot cake
10.Ghim Moh Chwee kueh
11.Margaret Drive Hokkien fried prawn mee
12. Muhammed Faisal roti Prata ( nice prate n curries)
13. Hin fried hor fun

LATEST as of SUNDAY FIRST MARCH 2026
If you like curry puffs, there’s a new stall at Ghim Moh Market & Hawker Centre, located next to the famous homemade fishball noodle stall( Thye hong).

They sell the flaky type of curry puffs with a variety of fillings such as sardine, yam and curry chicken with potato. I tried the chicken curry potato version yesterday morning.

The skin was crispy and nicely flaky, and not overly oily — that’s a plus. However, the curry chicken filling was only average and did not really stand out. It lacked fragrance and depth of flavour.

Overall, I would rate it as average for now.

⭐⭐⭐ (3/5)

reviews.author: Michelle
reviews.text: Amazingly fresh vegetables & all proteins, best organic chicken, fish and seafood. Was great to see they almost all speak English - incredible value! A must see for expats wanting to cook with fresh ingredients and hawker has some stunning classics & Michelin star

reviews.author: YL Wong
reviews.text:
The food center has pretty good food.  Eg:
Chee Cheong Fun, braised duck, fried carrot cake, whampoa bean curd, the roast meat stall etc…

reviews.author: TCH
reviews.text: Hor fan stall got quite a queue.  Tastewise got a bit of Wok Hey.  Fried Carrot Cake the Dark Sweet Sauce taste better!

reviews.author: C K Lee
reviews.text: Every hawker centre in Singapore has their hidden gem food stalls, usually known to some but unknown to many. This one however, has May stalls that are well known, and they happen to be some of my favourite dishes. Such as the braised duck rice stall. This most recent visit I found A Prawn Story…. A prawn noodle stall manned by young hawkers. I thoroughly enjoyed their noodles!

stall_name: Sheng Ji Shao La 昇记烧腊 (Ghim Moh Market)
reviews: Roasted Pork @ Sheng Ji Shao La 昇记烧腊 | 20 Ghim Moh Road | Ghim Moh Market & Food Centre #01-69. I noticed a relatively long queue for this roasted delight stall and decided to join the queue as well. I opted for the roasted pork meat only as I was not feeling that hungry. However, after taking a bite of the crackling and savory skin, it was simply irresistible that I can easily finish a whole bowl of white rice with this roasted meat.

other_info.address: 20 Ghim Moh Road #01-69 Ghim Moh Market Singapore 270020

reviews.author: OB1
reviews.text: This stall serves up good Roasted Pork and Char Siew with noodles or kway teow (烧肉叉烧面/河粉). The uncle running the stall is very polite and friendly. The meal is simple, filling, and very reasonably priced at just $4.50 for a generous portion of two meats. A great, quick lunch option!

reviews.author: Jason Lim
reviews.text: One of the best Wanton Noodles and Roast Meats 🥩 stalls in the area. 👍 Weekly fix almost every weekend. Noodle and Wanton is tasty - really old school. 👍 The Char Siew is also tender with traditional taste. 😋 Then there's the Roast Pork 🥩! Juicy and Tender and the skin is well marinated and roasted. 👍 Highly recommend Wanton Noodles and Roast Meat lovers to try this stall. 😊 👍

reviews.author: Wei Liang Sim
reviews.text:
I would say 5 ⭐ roast meat (siu yuk), but maybe 4 ⭐ wanton mee because it's a lean cut of char siew. Wanton is nice, noodles springy but alkaline.

Probably would go for the rice next time and spam extra roasted meat.

reviews.author: Karyn Ong
reviews.text: Super generous portions. Wanton skin is silky smooth and fillings are tasty. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Pauline Lim
reviews.text: The crispy crackling sound of the roast pork and not too salty taste with right ratio of meat and fats makes this one of the best in Sg. There are 3 stalls selling roast pork in Ghim Moh Food Centre, and this is the best. 6 dollars for such quantity.

reviews: Ice Kachang @ Golden Swallow Dessert, Ghim Moh Market & Food Centre #01-53. Plagued by a nasty cough...
Ice Kachang with Dad My grandparents used to stay in the Ghim Moh neighbourhood. This was where all the family gathered.

other_info.address: 20 Ghim Moh Road #01-34 Ghim Moh Market & Food Centre Singapore 270020

reviews.author: LP Pua
reviews.text: Ordered my usual ice jelly cocktail, and it met expectations—refreshing on a hot afternoon. However, it’s a bit expensive at $3.

reviews.author: Annie Ho
reviews.text:
Jackfruit ice kacang is 3sgd, so yummy.
Uncle and Auntie sell a lot of delicious desserts, it's fresh and cold.
Open 12pm - 6pm
Close on Sat, Sun, Mon every week.

reviews.author: AC Chan
reviews.text: Ordered a bowl of ice bubur cha cha from this popular dessert stall at Ghim Moh food centre that has all these gems — coconut 🥥 milk, sweet potato, yam and colourful tapioca cubes hidden under a mountain of finely shaved ice. 🫶🏻

reviews.author: Joshua Chew
reviews.text: One of the best Ice kachang experiences we've had! The spectacular dessert was incredibly affordable at just $1.70. The ice had a fine texture, reminiscent of Bingsu, and the flavors were perfectly balanced, not overly sweet. The bottom of the dessert featured a delightful assortment of jellies, including grass jelly and attap chee. The service was prompt, with barely any wait, and the auntie and uncle running the place were exceptionally friendly. Highly recommend and definitely planning to visit again!"

reviews.author: See Yanjun
reviews.text: The best ice kachang according to my mum. The gula melaka ice kachang is really good. It was so fragrant, even though it’s a bit too sweet for me.
